name: Meta Platforms
headline: Meta Platforms CARRY-FORWARD CONFIRMATION at 10.9 / Critical (working baseline 9.4 pending application from May 16 proposal) — May 17 is the pre-execution confirmation window for the May 20 8,000-person layoff. Internal memo confirmed May 17 but the underlying evidence (announcement, layoff details, severance terms, 6,000 cancelled requisitions, AI-pivot framing concurrent with $115-135B AI infrastructure spend) is materially unchanged from the May 16 proposal. EU DSA preliminary breach finding (Meta AI scope) and Meta's rejection of Commission findings are scored under the Meta AI ai-labs entity, not the Fortune 500 parent. No new May 17 entity-specific evidence beyond the pre-execution confirmation. Per 'do-not-re-propose-unless-new-material-evidence' protocol following yesterday's downgrade, hold the May 16 proposal in pending status. Forward flag: May 20 execution event will be the operative trigger for the next cycle. If May 16 proposal (9.4) is applied: confirm at 9.4; if not yet applied: apply from May 16 evidence package.
workingBaseline_note: Working baseline pending: May 16 proposal (10.9 → 9.4) not yet applied. This May 17 proposal does NOT re-propose a change; it confirms the carry-forward status pending May 16 application. Confidence MEDIUM: pre-execution window evidence does not add material weight to the May 16 proposal.

AWR.rationale: Sustained. May 17 internal memo confirms pre-existing awareness; no new dimensional weight.

EMP.rationale: Sustained. May 16 proposal already incorporated third-wave displacement pattern; pre-execution confirmation does not add weight.

ACC.rationale: Sustained at floor. EU DSA preliminary breach finding scored under Meta AI (ai-labs) per parent-vs-product-entity-scoping methodology.

SYS.rationale: Sustained. AI-pivot-displacement-wave sector pattern already incorporated in May 16 proposal.

evidence.summary: Internal memo May 17 confirms May 20 layoff of 8,000 employees (10% of 78,865-person workforce); layoffs hit Reality Labs, Facebook social division, recruiting, sales, global operations. 6,000 open requisitions cancelled (14,000 effective headcount reduction). Severance: 16 weeks base + 2 weeks/year + 18 months health coverage.
Outlook Business: detail on what employees will get after layoffs effective May 20. Confirms severance package.
May 16 change proposal (10.9 → 9.4) pending application. Pre-execution window May 17 does not add material new evidence beyond the May 16 evidence package.
247WallSt May 7 sector sweep: tens of thousands of tech workers laid off in 2026; $725B going to four companies (Amazon, Google, Meta, Microsoft). Pre-existing context, scored in May 16 proposal.

keyEvidence: May 17 internal memo confirms May 20 layoff details unchanged from prior announcement.
Per do-not-re-propose-unless-new-material-evidence protocol, hold May 16 proposal in pending status.
May 20 execution event will be operative trigger for next cycle.
If May 16 proposal (9.4) already applied: confirm at 9.4; if not applied: apply from May 16 evidence.
No new May 17 entity-specific evidence beyond pre-execution confirmation.
EU DSA scope continues to be scored under Meta AI (ai-labs), not Meta Platforms (Fortune 500).
rationale: Confirm at 10.9 (working baseline 9.4 pending May 16 application). The May 17 internal memo is a pre-execution confirmation of evidence already captured in the May 16 proposal — announcement, scale, severance terms, AI-pivot framing, sector context. No material new dimensional weight is available from a pre-execution confirmation alone. Per protocol governing recently-proposed entities, hold the proposal and flag May 20 execution as the next material event date. Math: (1.4+1.2+1.4+1.2+1.6+1.0+1.8+1.6)/8 = 11.2/8 = 1.4 → ((1.4-1)/4)*100 = 10.0. Note: published composite is stated as 10.9; reconstructed math from published dimensions yields 10.0. Math-hygiene flag for dimensional-vs-composite drift (0.9pt gap). Documented for reconciliation.
watches: May 20 execution: confirm actual layoff count, severance, affected region breakdown
EU DSA preliminary breach finding response trajectory (Meta AI scope — separate entity)
carryForward: Severance-package quality as sector-relative INT/EMP positive signal
Distinct scoping: Meta Platforms (fortune-500) vs Meta AI (ai-labs)
boundaryWatchResolution: Not a boundary case. Composite 10.9 (or working 9.4 pending) sits well within Critical band (0-20).

math_check.validator_check: Math drift flag: dimensional-sum-derived 10.0 vs published 10.9 (0.9pt gap). Carry-forward confirmation; no proposed change. Math-hygiene reconciliation flag documented for sub-blocking review.

disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
